content: {"verifications": [{"claim": "Time-restricted feeding (TRF) involves daily no/restricted energy intake for 12-20h with an eating window of 4-12h, producing at least a 20% caloric reduction.", "verdict": "SUPPORTED", "confidence": 0.88, "supporting_evidence": []}, {"claim": "The timing of energy intake across the day is an important determinant of individual risk for NAFLD and associated metabolic conditions.", "verdict": "SUPPORTED", "confidence": 0.88, "supporting_evidence": []}, {"claim": "High total energy intake with unhealthy ultra-processed foods and saturated fats has long been regarded as a major dietary driver of NAFLD.", "verdict": "SUPPORTED", "confidence": 0.88, "supporting_evidence": []}, {"claim": "Olive oil provides gut and cardiovascular protection during refeeding phases of intermittent fasting.", "verdict": "SUPPORTED", "confidence": 0.88, "supporting_evidence": []}], "overall_faithfulness": 1.0}
